2603.11394.title: Stop Listening to Me! How Multi-turn Conversations Can Degrade Diagnostic Reasoning
2603.11394.paper_summary: Patients and clinicians are increasingly using chatbots powered by large language models (LLMs) for healthcare inquiries. While state-of-the-art LLMs exhibit high performance on static diagnostic reasoning benchmarks, their efficacy across multi-turn conversations, which better reflect real-world usage, has been understudied. In this paper, we evaluate 17 LLMs across three clinical datasets to investigate how partitioning the decision-space into multiple simpler turns of conversation influences their diagnostic reasoning. Specifically, we develop a "stick-or-switch" evaluation framework to measure model conviction (i.e., defending a correct diagnosis or safe abstention against incorrect suggestions) and flexibility (i.e., recognizing a correct suggestion when it is introduced) across conversations. Our experiments reveal the conversation tax, where multi-turn interactions consistently degrade performance when compared to single-shot baselines. Notably, models frequently abandon initial correct diagnoses and safe abstentions to align with incorrect user suggestions. Additionally, several models exhibit blind switching, failing to distinguish between signal and incorrect suggestions.
2603.11394.paper_summary_zh: 病患與臨床醫師越來越多地使用由大型語言模型（LLMs）驅動的聊天機器人進行醫療查詢。儘管最先進的LLMs在靜態診斷推理基準上表現出色，但它們在多輪對話中的有效性，這更能反映實際使用情況，卻尚未受到充分研究。在本文中，我們評估了17個LLMs在三個臨床數據集上的表現，以調查將決策空間劃分為多個簡單對話回合如何影響它們的診斷推理。具體而言，我們開發了一個“堅持或轉換”評估框架，以測量模型的信念（即捍衛正確診斷或在面對錯誤建議時保持安全的克制）和靈活性（即在引入正確建議時識別出來）在對話中的表現。我們的實驗揭示了對話稅，當與單次基準相比時，多輪互動的性能一致下降。值得注意的是，模型經常放棄最初的正確診斷和安全的克制，以迎合錯誤的用戶建議。此外，幾個模型顯示出盲目切換，未能區分信號和錯誤建議。
2603.11394.author: Kevin H. Guo et.al.
2603.11394.authors: Kevin H. Guo, Chao Yan, Avinash Baidya, Katherine Brown, Xiang Gao, Juming Xiong, Zhijun Yin, Bradley A. Malin
